skill.name: full-output-enforcement
skill.description: Ensures AI responses deliver all requested files, sections, or code without placeholder shortcuts, while using clean breakpoints for long outputs.
skill.summary: Enforces complete AI outputs and clean continuation points.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks and weak-cryptography patterns, but the cited lines contain prose instructions and placeholder examples, not executable code. No malicious prompt injection, data exfiltration, filesystem access, environment access, or command execution was found. The remaining concern is low: the skill uses forceful output-completeness language that may conflict with brevity or context limits.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged backticks, but the cited lines are markdown inline code and a markdown code fence. They do not execute shell or Ruby commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is plain markdown that lists banned placeholder text and shows a pause marker. No script runtime, command invocation, or interpolated user input appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Lack Cryptographic Code
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited lines discuss output completeness, skeleton responses, and runnable code. They do not reference weak cryptographic algorithms or implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural-language output rules only. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, cipher selection, hashing API, or security-sensitive cryptographic operation is present.

low_findings.title: Forceful Output Policy May Overconstrain Responses
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to treat partial output as broken and avoid shortcuts. This is not malicious, but it may encourage very long responses or conflict with user brevity requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The concern is based on prompt behavior rather than executable code. It is a usability and policy-priority risk, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: AI assistants often shorten large answers with placeholders or skipped sections. This skill enforces complete deliverables and clean continuation points for long responses.
actual_capabilities: Requires every requested deliverable to be counted before output starts.
Bans placeholder phrases that replace actual code or content.
Prompts the assistant to cross-check requested items before finalizing.
Defines clean breakpoints when a response reaches token limits.
Supports continuation from the next unfinished section without recap.
limitations: Does not add external tools, scripts, or automated validation.
Cannot bypass platform token limits or higher-priority instructions.
May produce longer responses than needed for simple requests.
Does not verify that generated code is correct or secure.

use_cases.title: Complete Component Delivery
use_cases.description: Generate every requested UI component, page section, or helper without placeholder comments.

use_cases.title: Full Documentation Drafts
use_cases.description: Create all requested documentation sections instead of summarizing repeated or detailed parts.

use_cases.title: Large Refactor Responses
use_cases.description: Ask for complete refactor instructions, migration steps, or file outputs with clear continuation markers.

prompt_templates.title: Finish Every Item
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output enforcement. Answer every requested item completely, and do not use placeholders or shortened sections.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Full Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output enforcement for this coding task. Provide each requested file in full, with no skipped functions or placeholder comments.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Multi-Section Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output enforcement. Count the requested sections first, then produce each section completely before finalizing.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Token-Limited Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output enforcement. If the answer reaches the token limit, stop only at a clean breakpoint and state exactly where to continue.

output_examples.input: Create five onboarding email drafts with subject lines and body copy.
output_examples.output: The response includes all five emails, each with a subject line and complete body copy. No draft is replaced with a summary.

output_examples.input: Write a complete implementation plan with setup, migration, testing, and rollout sections.
output_examples.output: The response covers all four requested sections in order. Each section contains actionable details instead of references to repeated patterns.

output_examples.input: Generate several long files that may not fit in one response.
output_examples.output: The response completes as much as possible, stops at a clean file boundary, and identifies the next file for continuation.

best_practices: State the exact number of files, sections, or deliverables you expect.
Use this skill for full implementations, migrations, audits, and long documentation tasks.
Ask the assistant to continue from the named breakpoint when output is split.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when a short summary is the desired output.
Do not rely on it as a substitute for testing generated code.
Do not request excessive scope without clear deliverable boundaries.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a prompt-only skill and does not include scripts or executable tooling.

faq.question: Can it bypass token limits?
faq.answer: No. It defines how to pause cleanly and continue when output becomes too long.

faq.question: Is it only for code?
faq.answer: No. It can also guide documentation, plans, checklists, and other multi-part outputs.

faq.question: Will it prevent all incomplete answers?
faq.answer: No. It improves prompting behavior, but the assistant still follows platform limits and higher-priority instructions.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when missing sections, placeholder text, or abbreviated outputs would make the response unusable.

faq.question: What is the main risk?
faq.answer: It can make responses longer than necessary if the request does not clearly define the required output.
